created_by: Wilderculture WILD Toolbox session (Claude) — woodland group batch; field sheet 10 split per type. v3.3.

ukhab_identity.composite_pk_note: Single Level-4 type; PK = w2a. Native pinewood sits under Coniferous woodland (w2), distinct from non-native conifer (w2b Other Scots pine, w2c Other coniferous).

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01 (official definitions + BNG code list + applied surveys)
sources.specific_claim: w2 = Coniferous woodland; w2a = Native pine woodlands (Caledonian); w2b Other Scots pine; w2c Other coniferous. Native pinewood = Annex I H91C0 Caledonian forest.

cross_references.nvc_communities_notes: Caledonian Scots pine forest of the Highlands (Glen Affric, Abernethy, Loch Arkaig), with juniper and birch and its own specialist wildlife.
cross_references.bng_metric_habitat_name: Woodland and forest — Native pine woodlands

sources.title: NatureScot / Forestry & Land Scotland — Caledonian pinewood
sources.specific_claim: W18 Caledonian Scots pine forest with its specialist wildlife (capercaillie, crested tit, Scottish crossbill, red squirrel).

ecology.description: Caledonian Scots pine forest of the Highlands — Scots pine with birch and juniper on free-draining sands and podzols, more continental and eastern than the oceanic broadleaved woods. An internationally important native woodland with its own specialist wildlife. Managed by browsing level for regeneration, not stocked.
condition_indicators_good: Full age range incl. pine regeneration and standing/fallen deadwood (a key pinewood feature)
Native pine with birch, rowan and a juniper understorey
Intact ground flora — heather, bilberry, cowberry, mosses
Browsing light enough that pine seedlings survive
condition_indicators_poor: Overgrazed (deer): no pine regeneration, even-aged 'granny pines' with no heirs
ecosystem_functions: Internationally important native conifer forest with endemic-level specialists
Soil-building and long-term carbon store in soil and timber
Deadwood/saproxylic habitat and a distinct pinewood food web
Pine seed mast and a juniper understorey
ecology.whole_system_role: The native conifer climax of the eastern/continental Highlands — and, with its juniper and birch, the wooded top of that successional gradient.

successional_neighbours.note: Juniper and birch scrub nurse pine regeneration on acid ground when browsing is low (e.g. Ben Lawers pine/birch regeneration).
Grades up to montane scrub/heath at the treeline — the Cairngorms hold the UK's most natural treeline (Creag Fhiaclach).

sources.title: Wilderculture field sheet 10 — Native Woodland
sources.specific_claim: Caledonian pinewood, specialist wildlife, regeneration diagnostic.

ukhab_condition.condition_grade_note: Assigned per parcel; pine regeneration is the diagnostic.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Favourable
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Full age range with pine regeneration; juniper understorey; deadwood; native dominant.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable recovering
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Deer reduced; pine regeneration appearing; non-native conifer being removed.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable no change
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Mature pine, sparse regeneration; juniper declining.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable declining
condition_grade_descriptors.description: 'Granny pines' with no regeneration (deer overgrazing); non-native conifer ingress.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Destroyed / converted
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Replaced by plantation or lost with no continuity.

sources.title: Woodland Condition Assessment
sources.specific_claim: Regeneration and age structure are core indicators.

abiotic_template.climate: More continental and eastern than the oceanic broadleaved woods
abiotic_template.altitude_range_m: Valley floor up to the treeline (highest natural treeline in the Cairngorms)
abiotic_template.bioclimate_notes: Eastern Highlands above all — Glen Affric, Abernethy, Loch Arkaig, the Cairngorms.

elemental_conditions.convention_note: CURRENT-STATE reading. Protect & Restore (ancient Caledonian remnants irreplaceable); expansion = Bioregionally Wilder.

earth.descriptor: Acid podzols under pine; slow but real carbon storage in soil and long-lived timber.

fire.descriptor: High light capture and a long-lived carbon store; a distinct pinewood food web incl. deadwood specialists.

successional_envelope.current_stage: Climax (native conifer climax of the continental Highlands)
successional_envelope.successional_pathway: acid ground/heath → juniper-birch scrub → native pinewood (climax) → grades to montane at the treeline. NOT to displace deep peat or open priority habitats.

wild_assessment.energy_flow_role: The native conifer climax with a distinct specialist food web; a long-term carbon store and an internationally important woodland.
wild_assessment.wild_zone_rationale: Ancient Caledonian pinewood remnants are Protect & Restore — internationally important and irreplaceable, the seed sources and gene pool for expansion. New native pinewood expanding via reduced deer browsing is Bioregionally Wilder. Same 'right tree right place' exclusions apply.
wild_assessment.disturbance_regime: Browsing-controlled — DEER are the primary lever here. Reduce deer and pine/juniper/birch regenerate largely on their own where a seed source survives.

wild_management_target.detail: Achieve sustained pine and juniper regeneration by reducing deer; protect and expand ancient remnants; remove non-native conifer; keep deadwood and granny pines. Natural regeneration first, planting (native, local-provenance) only where seed source is gone. Mind juniper biosecurity (Phytophthora austrocedri).
integrity_indicators: capercaillie / crested tit / Scottish crossbill presence
red squirrel (and recovering pine marten tipping grey→red)

wild_assessment.ecological_value_notes: Among the very highest — internationally important Caledonian forest (H91C0) with specialists found almost nowhere else (capercaillie, crested tit, Scottish crossbill) and irreplaceable ancient remnants.

rarity_irreplaceability.notes: Ancient Caledonian remnants are rare and effectively irreplaceable; the gene pool for all expansion.

diversity.notes: Distinct specialist assemblage (birds, deadwood invertebrates, red squirrel).

energy_flow_contribution.notes: Full woodland energy flow plus a long-lived carbon store.

naturalistic_lu_ha.value: Near nil — reduce DEER to allow regeneration
naturalistic_lu_ha.basis_note: Deer management is the whole game in the pinewood; at Ben Lawers reduced grazing brought abundant pine/birch regeneration. Not a stocked habitat.

wild_assessment.mosaic_role_note: The native conifer climax; juniper/birch scrub nurse it. Not a grazing patch.
wild_assessment.preferential_grazing_magnet_note: Pine seedlings and juniper are browsed by deer — the reason regeneration fails at high deer density.
process_indicators: deer browse impact / non-native conifer removal
wild_assessment.process_indicators_note: Deer impact is the master variable; capercaillie is in genuine crisis.

sources.title: Wilderculture field sheet 10 — Native Woodland
sources.specific_claim: Pinewood ecology, deer as the lever, specialist wildlife, zone.

forage.overall_palatability_notes: Pine itself is low browse, but the juniper, birch and ground flora are browsed, and deer pressure prevents pine regeneration. Not a managed forage resource — the issue is deer, not stocking.

forage_yield_by_grade.notes: Not a forage/stocking habitat — managed by deer/browsing level for regeneration.

conservation_lu_ha.value: Near nil — reduce DEER to allow pine/juniper/birch regeneration
conservation_lu_ha.scheme: site-specific deer management; not a standard livestock stocking figure
conservation_lu_ha.basis_note: Deer management is the primary action; reduced grazing brings natural regeneration where a seed source survives (Ben Lawers). Not stocked with livestock.

sources.title: NatureScot / FLS Caledonian pinewood and deer management
sources.specific_claim: Deer reduction is the primary lever for pinewood regeneration.

forage.palatability_rank: Low (pine) — but juniper/birch/ground flora browsed; deer the constraint
forage.nutritional_quality_note: Soil-builder; no toxicity theme. The constraint is deer browsing of regeneration, not nutrition.

seasonal_forage_curve.spring: Ground flora and seedling browse (a regen risk)

optimal_grazing_animals.breed: None for production — manage DEER down
optimal_grazing_animals.notes: Deer management is the whole game; livestock are not the tool here.

unsuitable_animals.reason: Prevent pine and juniper regeneration — the chief threat to the pinewood.

timing_windows.preferred_season: Year-round deer management to a regeneration-permitting density
timing_windows.avoid: High deer density; non-native conifer planting
timing_windows.per_species_notes: Deer is the primary herbivore; livestock generally not used.

management.intensity_guidance: Reduce deer to a density that permits regeneration. Protect and expand ancient remnants; remove non-native conifer; keep granny pines and deadwood.
management.regulatory_note: Juniper faces Phytophthora austrocedri (root/collar rot, confirmed in Britain 2012, now widespread; UK uniquely has confirmed juniper infection) — BIOSECURITY matters: clean boots/tools/vehicles, careful stock sourcing, no moving potentially infected material; check current guidance before any juniper works. Capercaillie is in genuine crisis — manage sensitively (e.g. deer-fence collision risk). Many pinewoods are SSSI/SAC/SPA — check consents.
management.monitoring_protocol: Pine regeneration, age structure/granny pines, juniper understorey (and P. austrocedri), deadwood, non-native conifer, capercaillie and specialist presence, deer impact. Apply the re-grade trigger.

bng_unit_value.value_note: Very high distinctiveness — priority habitat (H91C0 Caledonian forest). Among the highest BNG values; ancient remnants irreplaceable.

sources.title: Statutory Biodiversity Metric
sources.specific_claim: Native pine woodland very high distinctiveness; confirm exact label.

key_positive_indicators: ground flora — heather, bilberry, cowberry, mosses
integrity_indicators_fauna: capercaillie (in genuine crisis — one of Britain's most threatened birds), crested tit, Scottish crossbill (found almost nowhere else)
black grouse on the edge; woodcock, woodpeckers
red squirrel (recovering pine marten tips grey→red); deadwood/saproxylic specialists
target_species_composition: multi-aged native pine with juniper/birch and regeneration
species_list.note: Skeleton only — full records in Phase 2B. Capercaillie sensitivity and juniper biosecurity are key management attributes; juniper also belongs to the scrub batch (h3/h1c6).

fields_fully_populated: ukhab_identity (w2a verified — under Coniferous woodland, distinct from non-native w2b/w2c)
deer as the management lever; juniper biosecurity; capercaillie crisis
fields_pending_research: Current juniper P. austrocedri biosecurity guidance — verify
data_completeness.notes: Completes the five-record woodland group. Internationally important Caledonian forest (H91C0); Protect & Restore for ancient remnants, Bioregionally Wilder for expansion. DEER are the management lever, not livestock. Juniper biosecurity (P. austrocedri) and capercaillie sensitivity flagged. Juniper also features in the scrub batch (next).

sources.title: NatureScot / FLS Caledonian pinewood
sources.specific_claim: W18 Caledonian forest; deer as the regeneration lever; specialist wildlife.
